title: Andrachne dregeana
taxonomy: Andrachne dregeana is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Andrachne. The genus Andrachne is part of the order Malpighiales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the division Magnoliophyta. It is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Andrachne dregeana is reported to have various medicinal uses. The plant is traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Its antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it effective in treating wounds and infections.

Its leaves and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Andrachne dregeana is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant is valued for its traditional use in folk medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Andrachne dregeana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
